behaviors.description: When a required key is absent from both file and environment, error message names the missing key by exact config path (e.g., 'database.host').
behaviors.justification: This is pure business logic: validate key presence and format error text. No actual file I/O or environment needed. Mock config data and verify exception message content. Real failure is an error that's vague ('Missing key') or omits the path—both caught by checking the exception text.
behaviors.test_description: Create a mock/stub config dict missing a required top-level key, pass it to the loader's validation function, catch the exception, and assert the error message contains the exact path 'database.host' (or equivalent required key).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test validates error message logic without file or environment; mocks are sufficient.

behaviors.description: When environment variable XDG_CONFIG_HOME is set, loader resolves config file path relative to that directory instead of ~/.config.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is path construction or file discovery: code might always use ~/.config, or join paths incorrectly, or fail to read from the actual custom directory. Mocking os.environ.get() alone does not verify the path is constructed correctly or that the file is actually found at that location. Must create temp directories and files on disk, set XDG_CONFIG_HOME, and verify the loader reads the correct file.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp directory, set XDG_CONFIG_HOME to point to it, create a config.yaml file there with known content, call the loader, and assert it loads the config from the custom directory (not ~/.config). Verify by reading the config object's loaded content.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires actual file system setup to catch path-resolution bugs; mocking would miss file-not-found or incorrect path joining.

behaviors.description: When an environment variable with TOOL_ prefix conflicts with a key in the config file, the environment variable value is used.
behaviors.justification: This is precedence logic: read two sources (file and environment), apply a rule (env overrides file), and return the result. No file I/O is required; mock both file reading and os.environ, verify the final config object uses the env value. Real failure is incorrect precedence (file overrides env) or env vars ignored—both caught by checking the loaded config dict.
behaviors.test_description: Mock file I/O to return config with 'database.host: file-value', mock os.environ to include 'TOOL_DATABASE_HOST=env-value', call the loader, and assert the loaded config has 'database.host: env-value'.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test validates precedence logic; no real files or environment needed.

behaviors.description: When the config file contains a YAML syntax error, the error message includes the line number where the problem was detected.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is the error message missing the line number or showing the wrong line. Line number comes from the real YAML parser (PyYAML, etc.), which only produces accurate line info when parsing actual YAML. Mocking yaml.load() or the YAML parser would hide failures in error message formatting or line number extraction. Must parse real invalid YAML to verify the error includes the correct line.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp file with invalid YAML (e.g., missing colon on line 3), call the loader on that file, catch the exception, and assert the error message includes 'line 3' (or the correct line number from the real parser).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires actual YAML parsing to catch error-message formatting bugs; mocks would pass even if line number is missing or wrong.

behaviors.description: When the config directory does not exist at startup, the loader creates it and writes a defaults file before proceeding.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is incomplete file system state: directory created but file not written, file written to wrong path, permissions prevent creation, or the loader errors instead of creating. Mocking os.makedirs() or open() would hide these failures. Must verify the actual directory and file exist on disk after the loader runs. Requires real file system operations.
behaviors.test_description: Start with a temp directory where the config subdirectory does not exist, call the loader with that temp directory, and assert the directory and defaults file actually exist on the file system and contain expected default content.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires verifying real files on disk; mocks would hide file creation failures or permission issues.
